sections.paragraph:
ῥητῶς (adv. of ῥητός ‘expressly stated’) pert. to what is stated or has been stated as being precisely so, expressly, explicitly (Aristot. 1017b, 1; 3; Stoic. III 219, 45; Polyb. 3, 23, 5; Plut., Brut. 997 [29, 4]; Diog. L. 8, 71; OGI 515, 39; SIG 685, 77 and 83; UPZ 110, 62 [164 b.c.]; POxy 237 VII, 7; Philo, Leg. All. 1, 60 al.; Jos., Ant. 1, 24, C. Ap. 1, 83; Just.) τὸ πνεῦμα ῥητῶς λέγει 1 Ti 4:1.—DELG s.v. εἴρω (2). M-M.

adv. adv. = adverb, adverbially

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

Aristot Aristot , various works, IV b.c.—List 5

Stoic Stoic , various dates—List 5

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Diog. L. Diog. L. = Diogenes Laertius, III a.d.—List 5

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

UPZ UPZ = Urkunden der Ptolemäerzeit—Lists 4, 6

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 905
